raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.1", "R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.5", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The excerpt contains explicit thesis (clarification-aware decoding), gap statement (NMT systems commit to single hypothesis), and a contribution claim ('first NMT system...'); a general review would reconstruct and assess all three.",
    "R1.2": "Multiple inferential chains are present—gate training, multi-task loss trade-off, entropy-like routing—and internal coherence across these is open to scrutiny, especially given the heavy hedging throughout.",
    "R1.3": "Specific metric claims (4.2 COMET gain, 2.8 chrF on lexically-ambiguous subset) are asserted; a general review would probe whether the experimental setup and reported numbers actually support the stated conclusions.",
    "R1.4": "The paper asserts 'to the best of our knowledge, the first NMT system' with a learned in-loop clarification mechanism; this strong novelty claim is assessable and would typically be a focal point of any general review.",
    "R1.5": "The structural ordering is non-standard: quantitative results appear before problem motivation, which arrives in paragraph three; a holistic review would flag this narrative-arc issue.",
    "R1.6": "The writing is saturated with cascaded hedges ('may suggest,' 'at least under the conditions tested,' 'could perhaps be characterized as,' 'tends to route,' 'in principle, ought to'); sentence-level quality is a highly salient issue in this excerpt."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should I provide a holistic review spanning all dimensions (contribution framing, logical consistency, evidence alignment, novelty, structure, and writing quality), or do you want me to focus on one specific concern—for example, the pervasive hedging and writing quality (R1.6), the strength of the novelty claim (R1.4), or the evidence-claim alignment (R1.3)?"
}

rationale.R1.1: The excerpt contains explicit thesis (clarification-aware decoding), gap statement (NMT systems commit to single hypothesis), and a contribution claim ('first NMT system...'); a general review would reconstruct and assess all three.
rationale.R1.2: Multiple inferential chains are present—gate training, multi-task loss trade-off, entropy-like routing—and internal coherence across these is open to scrutiny, especially given the heavy hedging throughout.
rationale.R1.3: Specific metric claims (4.2 COMET gain, 2.8 chrF on lexically-ambiguous subset) are asserted; a general review would probe whether the experimental setup and reported numbers actually support the stated conclusions.
rationale.R1.4: The paper asserts 'to the best of our knowledge, the first NMT system' with a learned in-loop clarification mechanism; this strong novelty claim is assessable and would typically be a focal point of any general review.
rationale.R1.5: The structural ordering is non-standard: quantitative results appear before problem motivation, which arrives in paragraph three; a holistic review would flag this narrative-arc issue.
rationale.R1.6: The writing is saturated with cascaded hedges ('may suggest,' 'at least under the conditions tested,' 'could perhaps be characterized as,' 'tends to route,' 'in principle, ought to'); sentence-level quality is a highly salient issue in this excerpt.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I provide a holistic review spanning all dimensions (contribution framing, logical consistency, evidence alignment, novelty, structure, and writing quality), or do you want me to focus on one specific concern—for example, the pervasive hedging and writing quality (R1.6), the strength of the novelty claim (R1.4), or the evidence-claim alignment (R1.3)?
